title: A Life of Resilience: Rose Girone
story: Rose Girone entered the world in 1912, destined for an extraordinary journey. Born in Janów, Austrian Poland on May 27, she would become a symbol of resilience. title: Blowing in the Wind: Airplane Testing Takes Flight
story: Whoosh! On May 27, 1931, engineers fired up a giant fan at Langley Field, Virginia. This wasn't just any fan - it was the first full-scale wind tunnel for testing airplanes in the United States. Like a massive hair dryer, it blew air over real planes to see how they'd handle flying conditions. This breakthrough helped make air travel safer and more efficient for everyone.

It was the largest fan ever built
It was made entirely of wood
activity_answer: It was the first full-scale one for testing airplanes

At the Italian Open, she powered through matches against Virginie Ruzici and Carling Basset before facing the legendary Chris Evert in the final. Maleeva's incredible stamina and skill led her to victory, beating Evert 6-3, 6-3. She became only the seventh player ever to defeat Evert on clay courts.

title: A Kiwi Rock Star Is Born
story: In a small New Zealand town, a future rock legend took his first breath. Neil Finn, the musical mastermind behind hits like 'Don't Dream It's Over', was born in Te Awamutu on May 27, 1958.
